verses.latin_vulgate: Væ provocatrix, et redempta civitas, columba !
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ה֥וֹי מֹרְאָ֖ה וְנִגְאָלָ֑ה הָעִ֖יר הַיּוֹנָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Woe to her that is filthy and polluted, to the oppressing city!

verses.latin_vulgate: non audivit vocem,  et non suscepit disciplinam ;  in Domino non est confisa,  ad Deum suum non appropinquavit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹ֤א שָֽׁמְעָה֙ בְּק֔וֹל לֹ֥א לָקְחָ֖ה מוּסָ֑ר בַּֽיהֹוָה֙ לֹ֣א בָטָ֔חָה אֶל־אֱלֹהֶ֖יהָ לֹ֥א קָרֵֽבָה׃
verses.king_james: She obeyed not the voice; she received not correction; she trusted not in the Lord; she drew not near to her God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Principes ejus in medio ejus quasi leones rugientes ;  judices ejus lupi vespere, non relinquebant in mane.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שָׂרֶ֣יהָ בְקִרְבָּ֔הּ אֲרָי֖וֹת שֹׁאֲגִ֑ים שֹׁפְטֶ֙יהָ֙ זְאֵ֣בֵי עֶ֔רֶב לֹ֥א גָרְמ֖וּ לַבֹּֽקֶר׃
verses.king_james: Her princes within her are roaring lions; her judges are evening wolves; they gnaw not the bones till the morrow.

verses.latin_vulgate: Prophetæ ejus vesani, viri infideles ;  sacerdotes ejus polluerunt sanctum,  injuste egerunt contra legem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נְבִיאֶ֙יהָ֙ פֹּֽחֲזִ֔ים אַנְשֵׁ֖י בֹּֽגְד֑וֹת כֹּהֲנֶ֙יהָ֙ חִלְּלוּ־קֹ֔דֶשׁ חָמְס֖וּ תּוֹרָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Her prophets are light and treacherous persons: her priests have polluted the sanctuary, they have done violence to the law.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dominus justus in medio ejus non faciet iniquitatem ;  mane, mane judicium suum dabit in lucem, et non abscondetur ;  nescivit autem iniquus confusionem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְהֹוָ֤ה צַדִּיק֙ בְּקִרְבָּ֔הּ לֹ֥א יַעֲשֶׂ֖ה עַוְלָ֑ה בַּבֹּ֨קֶר בַּבֹּ֜קֶר מִשְׁפָּט֨וֹ יִתֵּ֤ן לָאוֹר֙ לֹ֣א נֶעְדָּ֔ר וְלֹא־יוֹדֵ֥עַ עַוָּ֖ל בֹּֽשֶׁת׃
verses.king_james: The just Lord is in the midst thereof; he will not do iniquity: every morning doth he bring his judgment to light, he faileth not; but the unjust knoweth no shame.

verses.latin_vulgate: Disperdidi gentes,  et dissipati sunt anguli earum ;  desertas feci vias eorum,  dum non est qui transeat ;  desolatæ sunt civitates eorum,  non remanente viro, neque ullo habitatore.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִכְרַ֣תִּי גוֹיִ֗ם נָשַׁ֙מּוּ֙ פִּנּוֹתָ֔ם הֶחֱרַ֥בְתִּי חוּצוֹתָ֖ם מִבְּלִ֣י עוֹבֵ֑ר נִצְדּ֧וּ עָרֵיהֶ֛ם מִבְּלִי־אִ֖ישׁ מֵאֵ֥ין יוֹשֵֽׁב׃
verses.king_james: I have cut off the nations: their towers are desolate; I made their streets waste, that none passeth by: their cities are destroyed, so that there is no man, that there is none inhabitant.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixi : Attamen timebis me,  suscipies disciplinam ;  et non peribit habitaculum ejus,  propter omnia in quibus visitavi eam :  verumtamen diluculo surgentes corruperunt omnes cogitationes suas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָמַ֜רְתִּי אַךְ־תִּֽירְאִ֤י אוֹתִי֙ תִּקְחִ֣י מוּסָ֔ר וְלֹֽא־יִכָּרֵ֣ת מְעוֹנָ֔הּ כֹּ֥ל אֲשֶׁר־פָּקַ֖דְתִּי עָלֶ֑יהָ אָכֵן֙ הִשְׁכִּ֣ימוּ הִשְׁחִ֔יתוּ כֹּ֖ל עֲלִילוֹתָֽם׃
verses.king_james: I said, Surely thou wilt fear me, thou wilt receive instruction; so their dwelling should not be cut off, howsoever I punished them: but they rose early, and corrupted all their doings.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quapropter exspecta me, dicit Dominus,  in die resurrectionis meæ in futurum :  quia judicium meum ut congregem gentes,  et colligam regna,  et effundam super eos indignationem meam,  omnem iram furoris mei :  in igne enim zeli mei devorabitur omnis terra.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵ֤ן חַכּוּ־לִי֙ נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֔ה לְי֖וֹם קוּמִ֣י לְעַ֑ד כִּ֣י מִשְׁפָּטִי֩ לֶאֱסֹ֨ף גּוֹיִ֜ם לְקׇבְצִ֣י מַמְלָכ֗וֹת לִשְׁפֹּ֨ךְ עֲלֵיהֶ֤ם זַעְמִי֙ כֹּ֚ל חֲר֣וֹן אַפִּ֔י כִּ֚י בְּאֵ֣שׁ קִנְאָתִ֔י תֵּאָכֵ֖ל כׇּל־הָאָֽרֶץ׃
verses.king_james: Therefore wait ye upon me, saith the Lord, until the day that I rise up to the prey: for my determination is to gather the nations, that I may assemble the kingdoms, to pour upon them mine indignation, even all my fierce anger: for all the earth shall be devoured with the fire of my jealousy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia tunc reddam populis labium electum,  ut invocent omnes in nomine Domini,  et serviant ei humero uno.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי־אָ֛ז אֶהְפֹּ֥ךְ אֶל־עַמִּ֖ים שָׂפָ֣ה בְרוּרָ֑ה לִקְרֹ֤א כֻלָּם֙ בְּשֵׁ֣ם יְהֹוָ֔ה לְעׇבְד֖וֹ שְׁכֶ֥ם אֶחָֽד׃
verses.king_james: For then will I turn to the people a pure language, that they may all call upon the name of the Lord, to serve him with one consent.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ultra flumina Æthiopiæ, inde supplices mei ;  filii dispersorum meorum deferent munus mihi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵעֵ֖בֶר לְנַהֲרֵי־כ֑וּשׁ עֲתָרַי֙ בַּת־פּוּצַ֔י יוֹבִל֖וּן מִנְחָתִֽי׃
verses.king_james: From beyond the rivers of Ethiopia my suppliants, even the daughter of my dispersed, shall bring mine offering.

verses.latin_vulgate: In die illa non confunderis super cunctis adinventionibus tuis,  quibus prævaricata es in me,  quia tunc auferam de medio tui magniloquos superbiæ tuæ,  et non adjicies exaltari amplius in monte sancto meo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּיּ֣וֹם הַה֗וּא לֹ֤א תֵב֙וֹשִׁי֙ מִכֹּ֣ל עֲלִילֹתַ֔יִךְ אֲשֶׁ֥ר פָּשַׁ֖עַתְּ בִּ֑י כִּי־אָ֣ז ׀ אָסִ֣יר מִקִּרְבֵּ֗ךְ עַלִּיזֵי֙ גַּאֲוָתֵ֔ךְ וְלֹא־תוֹסִ֧פִי לְגׇבְהָ֛ה ע֖וֹד בְּהַ֥ר קׇדְשִֽׁי׃
verses.king_james: In that day shalt thou not be ashamed for all thy doings, wherein thou hast transgressed against me: for then I will take away out of the midst of thee them that rejoice in thy pride, and thou shalt no more be haughty because of my holy mountain.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et derelinquam in medio tui populum pauperem et egenum :  et sperabunt in nomine Domini.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהִשְׁאַרְתִּ֣י בְקִרְבֵּ֔ךְ עַ֥ם עָנִ֖י וָדָ֑ל וְחָס֖וּ בְּשֵׁ֥ם יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: I will also leave in the midst of thee an afflicted and poor people, and they shall trust in the name of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Reliquiæ Israël non facient iniquitatem,  nec loquentur mendacium,  et non invenietur in ore eorum lingua dolosa,  quoniam ipsi pascentur, et accubabunt,  et non erit qui exterreat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁאֵרִ֨ית יִשְׂרָאֵ֜ל לֹֽא־יַעֲשׂ֤וּ עַוְלָה֙ וְלֹֽא־יְדַבְּר֣וּ כָזָ֔ב וְלֹֽא־יִמָּצֵ֥א בְּפִיהֶ֖ם לְשׁ֣וֹן תַּרְמִ֑ית כִּי־הֵ֛מָּה יִרְע֥וּ וְרָבְצ֖וּ וְאֵ֥ין מַחֲרִֽיד׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: The remnant of Israel shall not do iniquity, nor speak lies; neither shall a deceitful tongue be found in their mouth: for they shall feed and lie down, and none shall make them afraid.

verses.latin_vulgate: Lauda, filia Sion ; jubila, Israël :  lætare, et exsulta in omni corde, filia Jerusalem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רׇנִּי֙ בַּת־צִיּ֔וֹן הָרִ֖יעוּ יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל שִׂמְחִ֤י וְעׇלְזִי֙ בְּכׇל־לֵ֔ב בַּ֖ת יְרוּשָׁלָֽ͏ִם׃
verses.king_james: Sing, O daughter of Zion; shout, O Israel; be glad and rejoice with all the heart, O daughter of Jerusalem.

verses.latin_vulgate: Abstulit Dominus judicium tuum ;  avertit inimicos tuos.  Rex Israël Dominus in medio tui :  non timebis malum ultra.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵסִ֤יר יְהֹוָה֙ מִשְׁפָּטַ֔יִךְ פִּנָּ֖ה אֹֽיְבֵ֑ךְ מֶ֣לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵ֤ל ׀ יְהֹוָה֙ בְּקִרְבֵּ֔ךְ לֹא־תִֽירְאִ֥י רָ֖ע עֽוֹד׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: The Lord hath taken away thy judgments, he hath cast out thine enemy: the king of Israel, even the Lord, is in the midst of thee: thou shalt not see evil any more.

verses.latin_vulgate: In die illa dicetur Jerusalem :  Noli timere ; Sion : Non dissolvantur manus tuæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּיּ֣וֹם הַה֔וּא יֵאָמֵ֥ר לִירוּשָׁלַ֖͏ִם אַל־תִּירָ֑אִי צִיּ֖וֹן אַל־יִרְפּ֥וּ יָדָֽיִךְ׃
verses.king_james: In that day it shall be said to Jerusalem, Fear thou not: and to Zion, Let not thine hands be slack.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dominus Deus tuus in medio tui fortis, ipse salvabit :  gaudebit super te in lætitia,  silebit in dilectione sua,  exsultabit super te in laude.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְהֹוָ֧ה אֱלֹהַ֛יִךְ בְּקִרְבֵּ֖ךְ גִּבּ֣וֹר יוֹשִׁ֑יעַ יָשִׂ֨ישׂ עָלַ֜יִךְ בְּשִׂמְחָ֗ה יַֽחֲרִישׁ֙ בְּאַ֣הֲבָת֔וֹ יָגִ֥יל עָלַ֖יִךְ בְּרִנָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: The Lord thy God in the midst of thee is mighty; he will save, he will rejoice over thee with joy; he will rest in his love, he will joy over thee with singing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nugas, qui a lege recesserant,  congregabo, quia ex te erant :  ut non ultra habeas super eis opprobrium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נוּגֵ֧י מִמּוֹעֵ֛ד אָסַ֖פְתִּי מִמֵּ֣ךְ הָי֑וּ מַשְׂאֵ֥ת עָלֶ֖יהָ חֶרְפָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: I will gather them that are sorrowful for the solemn assembly, who are of thee, to whom the reproach of it was a burden.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce ego interficiam omnes qui afflixerunt te in tempore illo :  et salvabo claudicantem,  et eam quæ ejecta fuerat congregabo :  et ponam eos in laudem, et in nomen,  in omni terra confusionis eorum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנְנִ֥י עֹשֶׂ֛ה אֶת־כׇּל־מְעַנַּ֖יִךְ בָּעֵ֣ת הַהִ֑יא וְהוֹשַׁעְתִּ֣י אֶת־הַצֹּלֵעָ֗ה וְהַנִּדָּחָה֙ אֲקַבֵּ֔ץ וְשַׂמְתִּים֙ לִתְהִלָּ֣ה וּלְשֵׁ֔ם בְּכׇל־הָאָ֖רֶץ בׇּשְׁתָּֽם׃
verses.king_james: Behold, at that time I will undo all that afflict thee: and I will save her that halteth, and gather her that was driven out; and I will get them praise and fame in every land where they have been put to shame.

verses.latin_vulgate: in tempore illo quo adducam vos,  et in tempore quo congregabo vos.  Dabo enim vos in nomen,  et in laudem omnibus populis terræ,  cum convertero captivitatem vestram coram oculis vestris,  dicit Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בָּעֵ֤ת הַהִיא֙ אָבִ֣יא אֶתְכֶ֔ם וּבָעֵ֖ת קַבְּצִ֣י אֶתְכֶ֑ם כִּֽי־אֶתֵּ֨ן אֶתְכֶ֜ם לְשֵׁ֣ם וְלִתְהִלָּ֗ה בְּכֹל֙ עַמֵּ֣י הָאָ֔רֶץ בְּשׁוּבִ֧י אֶת־שְׁבוּתֵיכֶ֛ם לְעֵינֵיכֶ֖ם אָמַ֥ר יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: At that time will I bring you again, even in the time that I gather you: for I will make you a name and a praise among all people of the earth, when I turn back your captivity before your eyes, saith the Lord.
